questions.text: The most common indication for cardiac catheterization is:

answers.text: Identification and assessment of coronary artery disease (CAD)

questions.text: The most common artery used for access during cardiac catheterization is the:
In the Judkins technique, the femoral artery is punctured how far below the inguinal ligament?
Which imaging modality provides real-time radiographic visualization during cardiac catheterization?
Compared to femoral access, radial artery access for cardiac catheterization:

answers.text: Reduces total recovery time but limits catheter size
Is preferred for pigtail catheter placement

questions.text: The Judkins Left (JL) catheter is specifically shaped to access the:
Which catheter is designed to allow larger volumes of contrast and is typically placed in the left ventricle?
Which statement about absolute contraindications to coronary angiography is correct?

answers.text: Active bleeding is an absolute contraindication
Elevated INR is an absolute contraindication

questions.text: Contrast agents used in cardiac angiography typically contain which element that may cause allergic reactions?
Conscious sedation for cardiac catheterization typically includes:
Invasive electrophysiology studies (EPS) are performed by:
Cardiac mapping is most useful for identifying all of the following EXCEPT:
Balloon angioplasty relieves coronary artery obstruction by:

answers.text: Stretching and tearing the plaque and vessel wall, displacing it to the sides
Delivering heat energy to destroy the plaque

questions.text: Stents are used in approximately what percentage of PCI procedures worldwide?
The rotablator rotational atherectomy system is primarily used to:
Radiofrequency (RF) ablation destroys targeted myocardial tissue by:

answers.text: Generating heat through the catheter tip

questions.text: The most commonly used isotope in radionuclide cardiac imaging is:
In radionuclide ventriculography, data recording is synchronized with which part of the ECG?
In synchronized cardioversion, the shock is timed to be delivered:

answers.text: On the R wave of the QRS complex

questions.text: Which rhythm is treated with UNSYNCHRONIZED shocks (defibrillation) rather than synchronized cardioversion?
If sense markers appear on the T wave instead of the R wave during setup for cardioversion, you should:

answers.text: Adjust the ECG size or select another lead

questions.text: The primary indication for a permanent pacemaker is:

answers.text: Prevention of sudden death from VT/VF
Relief or prevention of symptomatic bradycardia

questions.text: Why are bipolar pacemaker leads preferred over unipolar leads?

answers.text: They have better sensing, leading to better detection
They are less likely to be displaced

questions.text: Pacemaker oversensing can result in:

answers.text: Inappropriate pacing or shock delivery when no abnormal rhythm exists

questions.text: During pacemaker interrogation, what is the normal lead impedance range?
For a pacemaker, the pacing output should be set at:

answers.text: Twice the pacing threshold for voltage; three times threshold for pulse duration
Half the pacing threshold for voltage
Three times the pacing threshold for voltage; twice for pulse duration
